title: Weekly Training Volume Calculator | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Calculate Your Weekly Training Volume by Muscle Group
description: Find your optimal weekly training volume (MEV/MRV) by muscle group and fitness level. Free, fast & based on Israetel's framework.
intro: Effective strength training balances three fundamental principles: progressive overload, mechanical tension, and metabolic stress. Your weekly training volume—the total number of challenging sets you perform per muscle group—determines whether you'll progress, plateau, or overtrain. The Israetel framework defines MEV (Minimum Effective Volume) and MRV (Maximum Recoverable Volume) based on your muscle group and experience level. This calculator applies those evidence-based guidelines to help you train smarter. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly to stay scientifically accurate.
keyTakeaway: **Israetel MEV/MRV Framework**: Find the optimal weekly set range for each muscle group and training level. MEV is the minimum volume needed to drive progress; MRV is the maximum volume you can recover from without compromising performance or health.
useCases: Programming training splits by muscle group volume
Checking your current training volume against evidence-based guidelines
Quick reference for weekly workout programming

example.title: Sample Calculation
example.result: MEV 10 sets, MRV 20 sets per week

explanation:
## How Training Volume Works

Training volume is the total number of challenging sets you perform per muscle group each week. It's one of the most important variables determining whether your training drives muscle growth and strength gains.

## How We Calculate

This calculator applies the Israetel MEV/MRV framework based on your inputs: which muscle group and your experience level (beginner, intermediate, advanced). We provide evidence-based ranges that account for recovery capacity and how different muscles respond to training stimulus.

Muscle groups vary in size and recovery ability—legs tolerate higher volume than smaller muscles like biceps. Your experience level also affects your capacity: beginners recover more slowly and need less weekly volume, while advanced lifters can handle more.

## Final Notes

These values are guidelines, not prescriptions. Individual recovery depends on sleep, nutrition, stress, and genetics. For significant training changes, consult a qualified strength coach. Values are current as of April 2026 and reviewed periodically as strength science evolves.

faq.q: What is MEV in strength training?
faq.a: MEV (Minimum Effective Volume) is the smallest weekly volume needed to trigger muscle growth and strength gains. Less than this and your training won't produce meaningful results.

faq.q: What is MRV in strength training?
faq.a: MRV (Maximum Recoverable Volume) is the upper limit of weekly volume you can handle while still recovering properly. Exceeding this increases fatigue, reduces performance, and raises injury risk.

faq.q: Why do different muscle groups have different volume recommendations?
faq.a: Muscle groups vary in size, fiber composition, and recovery ability. Your legs contain massive amounts of muscle and tolerate high weekly volume. Smaller muscles like biceps have less capacity and fatigue faster.

faq.q: How does fitness level affect training volume recommendations?
faq.a: Beginner lifters have lower work capacity and recover slowly—they need less volume per week. Advanced lifters have built up tolerance through years of training and can handle more weekly volume.

faq.q: How do I use MEV and MRV in my actual training?
faq.a: Start a training block near MEV and gradually increase volume as you progress, staying under MRV. This progressive approach (called 'undulating volume') drives adaptation while protecting your recovery.

faq.q: Is this calculator accurate for my situation?
faq.a: Our calculations use the Israetel framework, which is research-validated. Results are evidence-based guidelines, not personalised prescriptions. Your individual recovery depends on sleep, nutrition, stress, and genetics.

faq.q: Do you collect or store my data?
faq.a: No. All calculations run in your browser. We don't collect, store, or transmit any of your information to our servers. Your privacy is completely protected.
Yes. All Hacé Cuentas calculators are completely free—no paywalls, no ads, no account signup required.
